headline: Round 7 simplifies the tray's front wall per modeler-notes-v7. The round-6 variable-height profile (corners at z=18, lowered center at z=10, transition arcs r=8, side fillets r=12) is collapsed to a single uniform front_wall_h=10 across the full width, with ONE concave fillet (r=20) per side handling the entire side-wall (z=30) to front-wall (z=10) transition. No corner bars, no intermediate flat sections, no sharp points. r=2 top-edge fillet on back/sides + r=0.8 on the front wall (smaller because the front wall is only 1.6 mm thick — r=2 collapses). Cradle untouched.

dimensions.notes: ZERO CHANGES from round 6. STL re-rendered to confirm cradle.scad untouched.

Round-7 simplification on tray-only. Mesh dimensions identical to round 6 (X 103.2, Y 94.2, Z 29.948). Volume slightly higher than round 6 (38.90 vs 38.49 cm³) because the front wall has more material (uniform z=10 across full width vs round 6's variable-height which had less material at the lowered center transitions). Face count drops 13252 → 9010 (32% fewer faces) because the front-wall-top cutter polygon is much simpler — 2 quarter arcs instead of 4 arcs + corner flats + transitions.

totals.change_breakdown: Tray: +0.41 cm³ (uniform front wall has slightly more material than the round-6 variable profile). Cradle: unchanged.

fix1_front_wall_uniform_z10.description: Round-6's variable-height front wall (corners z=18, center z=10, transition arcs r=8, side fillets r=12) is replaced with a single uniform front_wall_h=10 across the full width. NO corner sections at z=18, NO transition arcs, NO variable-height profile. Front wall reads as a clean low band across the entire user-front face.
spec_diffs: UPDATE: tray_front_wall_construction (description rewritten for uniform-height + single-fillet construction)
UPDATE: front_wall_top_z_above_desk (single value 21, was dual corner=29/center=21)
UPDATE: testPrintCandidates entry for the front wall (uniform low + side fillet sweep)

fix2_single_concave_side_fillet_r20.scope: encoded in the simplified front-wall-top cutter polygon
fix2_single_concave_side_fillet_r20.description: ONE concave quarter-arc fillet per side, r=20, sweeping continuously from side-wall top (z=30) to front-wall top (z=10) over the 20mm height drop. Single curve, single radius, no intermediate flat section, no transition arc. Geometric construction: arc center at (wall_t, front_wall_h)=(1.6, 10) for the left side, sweeping 0°→90° CCW; mirror for right side. Concave-from-above (wall material bulges up into the cut region; user perceives a soft scoop).

fix3_top_edge_fillet_r0_8_on_front_wall.description: r=2 top-edge fillet roll continues on back + side walls (unchanged from round 6). A separate r=0.8 fillet stack rolls the front wall top at z=10. Constructed as a separate slab-stack restricted via a front_top_mask to the front wall slab in the X range BETWEEN the two side-fillet sweeps (x = wall_t + r_side .. ext_w - wall_t - r_side = 21.6 .. 81.6). The r=0.8 deviation from r=2 is documented per modeler-notes-v7 §Uncertain — the front wall is 1.6 mm thick and r=2 would exceed half the wall thickness, collapsing the offset.

deviations.what: Front wall top fillet uses r=0.8, not r=2
deviations.why: Front wall thickness is 1.6 mm. An r=2 fillet attempts to round the top inward by 2mm — that exceeds half the wall thickness and the slab-stack's offset(r=-inset) shrinks the rounded_rect footprint by more than the wall's half-thickness, collapsing the front wall material at the topmost layers. r=0.8 is half the wall thickness — the offset shrinks the footprint by at most 0.8mm, leaving a 0.8mm-wide top surface at the topmost layer (the offset starts at 0 and grows to 0.8 across the fillet stack). This is the deviation explicitly anticipated in modeler-notes-v7 §Uncertain. Alternative considered: skip the top fillet entirely on the front wall (leave a sharp top edge); rejected because the top edge would not match the back/sides' soft round.

deviations.what: Y-direction step at y = ext_d - wall_t (carried over from round 6)
deviations.why: The front-wall-top cutter is extruded along Y across only the front-wall slab thickness (y = ext_d - wall_t .. ext_d). Outside that slab (y < ext_d - wall_t), the side wall stays at full z=30. The transition between the side-wall material at z=30 (back of slab) and the side-fillet sweep down to z=10 (front of slab) is a Y-direction discontinuity at y=92.6. Same as round 6's documented simplification. Visually mostly hidden because the side wall top is itself rolled by the r=2 top-edge fillet. Modeler-notes-v7 didn't flag this for fixing, so left as-is. If round-8 critique flags it, the fix is a double-curvature spherical fillet (r=20 in X plus r=2 in Y) at the upper outer corners.

fix4_interior_ramp_unchanged.description: Interior parabolic floor ramp z(y) = 1.6 + 8.4 * ((y - 62.6) / 30)^2 unchanged from round 6. Endpoint at z=10 still equals front_wall_h, just renamed (was front_wall_center_h, now front_wall_h). Param wiring updated: ramp_front_z = front_wall_h.

specDeviations.reason: Fix 1: front wall is uniform z=10 across the full width. No more variable-height profile.

specDeviations.round_6_value: 12 (concave fillet z=30→18) + 8 (transition arc z=18→10) — two fillets in series
specDeviations.reason: Fix 2: ONE continuous concave quarter-arc per side replaces the round-6 two-fillets-in-series. Radius matches the 20mm height drop for a clean quarter circle.

specDeviations.round_6_value: n/a (no separate front-wall top fillet — the variable arcs were the treatment)
specDeviations.reason: Fix 3: with the front wall now uniform-height, a separate top-edge roll fillet is added. r=0.8 instead of r=2 because the front wall is only 1.6 mm thick — r=2 would collapse the offset. Documented deviation per modeler-notes-v7 §Uncertain.

Concave side fillet sweeps from higher z (z=30 at side) to lower z (z=10 at front) in the X direction — every layer has support below. Concave parabolic ramp self-supporting (unchanged). No supports needed.

features.name: base_plate
features.description: Stepped base plate with r=10 hero corner radius. UNCHANGED from round 5/6.

features.name: low_wall_block
features.description: 25 mm tall stepped perimeter wall block. UNCHANGED from round 5/6.

features.name: printer_shelf_concave_fillet
features.description: r=10 concave quarter-arc on both ±X sides where the 86 mm printer section transitions to the 110 mm shelf section. UNCHANGED from round 5/6.

features.name: host_object_proxy
features.description: RENDER-ONLY printer proxy box. UNCHANGED from round 5/6.

featuresTray.name: tray_floor
featuresTray.description: Solid floor at z = 0..floor_t (0..1.6 mm) at the back portion of the bin (y = wall_t..ramp_back_y = 1.6..62.6). Full interior width (x = wall_t..ext_w-wall_t = 1.6..101.6). UNCHANGED from round 6.

featuresTray.name: tray_walls_back_sides
featuresTray.description: Back wall (-Y) and ±X side walls — vertical, rounded_rect outer outline with r=3 vertical edge fillet, uniform 1.6 mm thickness. Top edges (z = ext_h = 30) carry a continuous r=2 quarter-arc fillet rolled inward via 24-step slab stack. UNCHANGED from round 5/6.

featuresTray.name: tray_front_wall_uniform
featuresTray.description: ROUND-7 SIMPLIFICATION. Uniform-height front wall at z=front_wall_h=10 across the entire width (x = wall_t+r_side..ext_w-wall_t-r_side = 21.6..81.6 in the flat segment between the two side fillets; full x-range x=wall_t..ext_w-wall_t when including the side fillet sweeps). Material at y in [ext_d-wall_t, ext_d] = [92.6, 94.2]. Replaces round-6's variable-height profile. THE GRAB FEATURE: user reaches over the 10mm-tall front wall ANYWHERE along its width to grab the lip or retrieve labels. r=0.8 top-edge fillet rolls the flat-segment top inward (limited by 1.6mm wall thickness — r=2 would collapse).

featuresTray.name: side_wall_to_front_wall_fillets
featuresTray.description: ROUND-7 SIMPLIFICATION. ONE concave quarter-arc per side, r=20, sweeping continuously from side-wall top (z=30) to front-wall top (z=10) over the 20mm height drop. Single curve, single radius, no intermediate flat sections. Geometric construction: arc center at (wall_t, front_wall_h)=(1.6, 10) for the left, 0°→90° CCW sweep; mirrored on the right. Concave-from-above. Replaces round-6's two-fillets-in-series (r=12 corner fillet z=30→18 + r=8 transition arc z=18→10). Encoded into the front-wall-top cutter polygon as left_side_fillet and right_side_fillet arcs. Operates only within the front-wall slab (y in [ext_d-wall_t, ext_d]); a Y-direction step at y=ext_d-wall_t is a documented simplification carried over from round 6.

featuresTray.name: interior_floor_ramp
featuresTray.description: Concave parabolic floor surface. Curve z(y) = 1.6 + 8.4 * ((y-62.6)/30)^2 connects (y=62.6, z=1.6) (back end, tangent to flat floor) to (y=92.6, z=10) (front end, at front-wall top). UNCHANGED from round 6 — endpoint at z=10 still matches front_wall_h.

cradle.bed_face: base plate bottom (z = 0 — flush with bed, NO feet)

tray.bed_face: floor (z = 0 on bed) — face up (open top up)
tray.notes: Round 7 confirms face-up. Uniform front wall is a simple closed perimeter — fully self-supporting. ONE concave side fillet per side sweeps from higher z (z=30 at the outer corner) to lower z (z=10 at the front-wall top) in the X direction — every layer has support below. Concave parabolic ramp surface (unchanged) curves DOWN from the floor surface — no overhangs at any layer. r=0.8 front-wall top fillet rolls inward — no overhangs. No supports.

userOrientationConfirmed.user_front: +Y face (low front lip — grab anywhere along its width)

render_method: cli-anything-openscad render png with explicit gimbal cameras (tx,ty,tz,rx,ry,rz,d). Combined-cradle-tray harness files (.combined-bare.scad, .combined-use.scad) used for views that show both parts in the same frame. Tray-only views use tray.scad directly. Render quality DRAFT throughout ($fn=100, top_fillet_steps=24, ramp_arc_steps=32).

front_wall_uniform_z10_band.details: tray-user-front shows a single horizontal line at z=10 across the full width of the front wall top, with concave fillet sweeps rising to z=30 at the sides. NO corner bars, NO sharp points, NO variable-height profile. Reads as a clean low band — exactly what was requested in the user-feedback turn 11.

single_concave_side_fillet_no_intermedia.details: tray-user-front-threequarter and cradle-user-front-threequarter both show a single smooth quarter-arc sweep from each side wall top (z=30) curving down to the uniform front wall top (z=10). No intermediate flat z=18 section, no transition arcs, no kink. The r=20 sweeps look generous and friendly.

no_sharp_points.details: All inspected views (tray-user-front, tray-user-front-threequarter, cradle-user-front, cradle-user-front-threequarter, cradle-user-top-threequarter) show no sharp protrusions in the front wall geometry. The visible Y-direction discontinuity at y=ext_d-wall_t (where the front-wall slab cutter terminates) is soft (r=2 top-edge fillet on side wall + r=20 concave sweep) and reads as a smooth transition, not a sharp point. Same documented simplification as round 6.

front_wall_top_fillet_r0_8_works.details: r=0.8 front-wall top fillet renders cleanly. Watertight check passes (is_watertight: true). The deviation from r=2 to r=0.8 was anticipated in modeler-notes-v7 §Uncertain (front wall is 1.6 mm thick — r=2 exceeds half the wall thickness and would collapse). r=0.8 leaves a soft round on the front lip without offset failure.

interior_ramp_unchanged.details: Quadratic ramp z(y) = 1.6 + 8.4 * ((y-62.6)/30)^2 unchanged from round 6. Tangent to flat floor at the back, terminates at z=10 at the front wall interior face. Self-supporting in face-up print orientation.

cradle_zero_changes.details: cradle.scad untouched. STL re-rendered to verify: dimensions identical (110 × 254.9 × 25). All round-3, 4, 5, 6 cradle work preserved.

uncertainItemsFromV7Notes.front_wall_uniform_height_z10: Reads cleanly in the renders. The 10mm tall front wall feels right for a closed-bin tray — clearly a 'low lip' but tall enough to contain dropped labels. If round-8 critique flags as too short, bump to 12; if too tall, drop to 8. Current verdict: pass.
uncertainItemsFromV7Notes.side_fillet_r20: The r=20 sweep matches the 20mm height drop from z=30 to z=10 — produces a clean quarter circle. Reads as 'gentle and ergonomic' in renders, not 'too generous.' Consumes 20mm of side-wall material at the front (x=wall_t to wall_t+20=21.6, mirrored). Modeler stuck with r=20 (not r=15) because the larger radius reads as friendlier and matches the height drop exactly. If round-8 critique flags as too generous, drop to r=15.
uncertainItemsFromV7Notes.r2_top_edge_fillet_on_front_wall_collaps: Confirmed: r=2 collapses on a 1.6mm wall (the offset(r=-inset) shrinks the footprint by up to 2mm at the topmost layer, exceeding half the wall thickness). Modeler used r=0.8 (half the wall thickness) for the front wall top fillet. Slab-stack assembles cleanly with this radius. Documented deviation.

renderTimeRecord.comparison_note: Tray STL is FASTER than round 6 (12s vs 16s) due to the simpler front-wall-top cutter polygon (2 arcs vs 4 arcs + corner flats). Face count drops 13252 → 9010 (32% fewer faces). Cradle render time unchanged (untouched scad).
